corpus_building.implemented_as: The TXT paper is converted into a section-aware annotated mini-corpus.

tokenization.implemented_as: Every concept phrase and sentence is normalized into tokens for counts and n-grams.

normalization.implemented_as: Lower-casing and punctuation cleanup are used for phrase matching and deduplication.

n_gram.implemented_as: Concept length and document top unigrams/bigrams/trigrams are exposed.

bow_and_naive_bayes.implemented_as: Section BoW hits act as class-conditional section evidence for intro/method/experiment/conclusion style decisions.

tf_idf_document_term_matrix.implemented_as: Document-level frequency and TF-IDF matrices expose which section concepts are characteristic of this paper.

laplace_smoothing.implemented_as: P(section | concept) uses add-one smoothing, so unseen section-concept pairs are not forced to zero.

evidence_cue_lexicon.implemented_as: Cue phrases such as propose, achieve, outperform, and BLEU give transparent rule-based evidence hints.

sequence_labeling.implemented_as: The first model contributes BIO boundary evidence for concept phrase extraction.

canonicalization_noisy_channel_style.implemented_as: Observed surface phrases are mapped to canonical terms with an alias confidence score.

location.sentence: In this paper, we embrace this observation and introduce the Dense Convo- lutional Network (DenseNet), which connects each layer to every other layer in a feed-forward fashion.

context_window.previous: Recent work has shown that convolutional networks can be substantially deeper, more accurate, and efficient to train if they contain shorter connections between layers close to the input and those close to the output.
context_window.current: In this paper, we embrace this observation and introduce the Dense Convo- lutional Network (DenseNet), which connects each layer to every other layer in a feed-forward fashion.
context_window.next: Whereas traditional convolutional networks with L layers have L connections—one between each layer and its subsequent layer—our network has L ( L +1) 2 direct connections.

top_candidates.sentence: To highlight general trends, we mark all results that outperform the existing state-of-the-art in boldface and the overall best result in blue.
To highlight general trends, we mark all results that outperform the existing state-of-the-art in boldface and the overall best result in blue.

Possibly the most noticeable trend may originate from the bottom row of Table , which shows that DenseNet-BC with L = 190 and k = 40 outperforms the existing state-of-the-art consistently on all the CIFAR datasets.

Possibly the most noticeable trend may originate from the bottom row of Table , which shows that DenseNet-BC with L = 190 and k = 40 outperforms the existing state-of-the-art consistently on all the CIFAR datasets.
For example, our 250-layer model only has 15.3M parameters, but it consistently outperforms other models such as FractalNet and Wide ResNets that have more than 30M parameters.

We also highlight that DenseNet-BC with L = 100 and k = 12 achieves comparable performance (e.g., 4.51% vs 4.62% error on C10+, 22.27% vs 22.71% error on C100+) as the 1001-layer pre-activation ResNet using 90% fewer parameters.
We evaluate DenseNet-BC with different depths and growth rates on the ImageNet classification task, and compare it with state-of-the-art ResNet architectures.

The results presented in the figure reveal that DenseNets perform on par with the state-of-the-art ResNets, whilst requiring significantly fewer parameters and computation to achieve comparable performance.
We plan to study such feature transfer with DenseNets in future work.
We empirically demonstrate DenseNet's effectiveness on several benchmark datasets and compare with state-of-theart architectures, especially with ResNet and its variants.
We denote this data augmentation scheme by a "+" mark at the end of the dataset name (e.g., C10+).
In this paper, we embrace this observation and introduce the Dense Convolutional Network (DenseNet), which connects each layer to every other layer in a feed-forward fashion.
We evaluate our proposed architecture on four highly competitive object recognition benchmark tasks (CIFAR-10, CIFAR-100, SVHN, and ImageNet).
We evaluate our proposed architecture on four highly competitive object recognition benchmark tasks (CIFAR-10, CIFAR-100, SVHN, and ImageNet).
DenseNets obtain significant improvements over the state-of-the-art on most of them, whilst requiring less computation to achieve high performance.

In this paper, we propose an architecture that distills this insight into a simple connectivity pattern: to ensure maximum information flow between layers in the network, we connect all layers (with matching feature-map sizes) directly with each other.

In this paper, we propose an architecture that distills this insight into a simple connectivity pattern: to ensure maximum information flow between layers in the network, we connect all layers (with matching feature-map sizes) directly with each other.
Our proposed DenseNet architecture explicitly differentiates between information that is added to the network and information that is preserved.
We evaluate DenseNets on four highly competitive benchmark datasets (CIFAR-10, CIFAR-100, SVHN, and ImageNet).
Our models tend to require much fewer param-eters than existing algorithms with comparable accuracy.
Further, we significantly outperform the current state-ofthe-art results on most of the benchmark tasks.
Further, we significantly outperform the current state-ofthe-art results on most of the benchmark tasks.
Parallel to our work, derived a purely theoretical framework for networks with cross-layer connections similar to ours.
ResNets with pre-activation also facilitate the training of state-of-the-art networks with > 1000 layers .
There are other notable network architecture innovations which have yielded competitive results.

downstream_contract.grounding_rule: The downstream generator should write from evidence_units.location.sentence and context_window, not from unsupported outside knowledge.
